description: Single-pane NOC view: dual-WAN uplink quality, Cloudflare tunnel edge, interface throughput, DNS/Pi-hole, per-VLAN firewall, service reachability and path health. Generated by scripts/grafana/build_internet_network_overview.py.

panels.description: Note: WAN2_DHCP dpinger monitors 192.168.12.1 (the ISP-modem next-hop on the LAN, ~1ms), not an external host — it reflects modem reachability only. WAN1GW monitors 8.8.8.8 (external).
panels.title: WAN2 RTT

panels.description: Note: WAN2_DHCP dpinger monitors 192.168.12.1 (the ISP-modem next-hop on the LAN, ~1ms), not an external host — it reflects modem reachability only. WAN1GW monitors 8.8.8.8 (external).
panels.title: WAN2 Loss

panels.description: Note: WAN2_DHCP dpinger monitors 192.168.12.1 (the ISP-modem next-hop on the LAN, ~1ms), not an external host — it reflects modem reachability only. WAN1GW monitors 8.8.8.8 (external).
panels.title: Packet Loss (%)

panels.description: Per-WAN uptime over the dashboard window: fraction of dpinger samples with loss<100% (WAN2 reflects ISP-modem reachability, not the open internet).
panels.title: Per-WAN availability % (window)

panels.description: Counts up/down transitions and draws a DOWN band whenever a WAN gateway hits 100% loss (the pfSense failover trigger condition) within the window.
panels.title: WAN failover events (100%-loss down intervals)

panels.description: Percentage of window samples where gateway RTT exceeded the 80ms SLO the live RTT panel already uses as its red threshold.
panels.title: WAN latency-SLO breach % (delay>80ms, window)

panels.description: Uplink saturation as a fraction of link capacity (ifHighSpeed: WAN1=2500, WAN2=1000 Mbit/s), the actionable framing the absolute-bps throughput panel lacks.
panels.title: WAN link utilization vs capacity (%)

panels.description: RTT-normalized jitter (stddev relative to the mean) that surfaces relative link instability the absolute-ms jitter panel hides; ratio>1 means stddev exceeds the mean.
panels.title: WAN jitter ratio (stddev / RTT)

panels.description: Consolidated WAN1-vs-WAN2 row table (RTT, current loss, window availability) joined on the gateway label — the explicit side-by-side comparison the dimension asks for.
panels.title: WAN1 vs WAN2 — SLA comparison
🟧 Cloudflare Tunnel / Edge — public ingress health

panels.description: Worst-replica count of HA connections cloudflared holds to the Cloudflare edge; 4 is healthy, 0 means the bologna tunnel is down and all *.cf.lcamaral.com is offline.
panels.title: 🟧 Tunnel edge connections (min HA)

panels.description: Number of cloudflared daemon replicas (of 3) currently scraping/alive; distinct from HA connections since a daemon can be up but disconnected from the edge.
panels.title: Cloudflared replicas up

panels.description: Real inbound Cloudflare traffic broken into status classes (collapsing 16 distinct codes); a sustained 5xx rate is origin errors through the tunnel that synthetic blackbox probes may miss, and total QPS doubles as the public-traffic-volume view.
panels.title: Edge responses by HTTP status class (/s)

panels.description: Fraction of tunnel requests cloudflared failed to proxy to the origin; clamp_min keeps it numeric during idle windows and catches a degraded-but-up ingress (edge connected, rproxy flaky).
panels.title: Tunnel error ratio %

panels.description: Per-replica HA connection count over time so a single flapping connector can be distinguished from a full-tunnel edge outage and correlated with WAN/failover events.
panels.title: Tunnel HA connections per replica

panels.description: Per-replica tunnel-path latency to the Cloudflare edge over QUIC (values already in ms, do NOT scale); rising QUIC RTT with healthy WAN RTT points to an edge-path/ISP-peering problem the gateway RTT cannot see.
panels.title: QUIC RTT to Cloudflare edge (ms)

panels.description: Edge-path packet loss summed across the 4 QUIC conns per replica (collapsing the timeout/reordering reason label); captures congestion on the tunnel's UDP/443 route that the pfSense gateway loss_ratio cannot, since QUIC takes a different path.
panels.title: QUIC packet loss on tunnel uplink (/s)

panels.description: Per-VLAN link saturation as a fraction of the negotiated ifHighSpeed capacity (10G on the ix0 VLANs, 2.5G/1G on the igc WANs) — answers 'how full is the link?' which the absolute-bps panels never do.
panels.title: Interface utilization % vs link capacity

panels.description: Errors and discards normalized by total inbound packets (ucast+mcast+bcast) so identical error counts on idle vs busy links are not confused — the existing panel only shows absolute pps.

targets.expr: rate(ifInErrors{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) / (rate(ifHCInUcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInMulticastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInBroadcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval])) * 100

rate(ifInDiscards{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) / (rate(ifHCInUcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInMulticastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInBroadcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval])) * 100

panels.title: Errors & discards as % of inbound packets (per VLAN)

panels.description: Per-VLAN link state from two independent sources — pfSense-native exporter (friendly names, 1=up/0=down) and the SNMP routing plane (ifOperStatus, 1=up/2=down) — disagreement between the two rows flags exporter/SNMP drift; there is no link-state view anywhere today.
panels.title: Interface up/down — physical link state

panels.description: Total packet rate (ucast+mcast+bcast) per VLAN in each direction — exposes small-packet floods (SYN storms, broadcast loops) that saturate the forwarding path without moving the bytes/s graphs.

targets.expr: rate(ifHCInUcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInMulticastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInBroadcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval])

rate(ifHCOutUcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCOutMulticastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCOutBroadcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval])

panels.title: Packet rate (pps) per VLAN

panels.description: Share of inbound packets that are broadcast/multicast per VLAN — a rising share is an early L2-loop / mDNS-storm indicator; live data already shows GUEST ~41%, ADMIN ~26%, WAN2 ~15% vs HOME ~0.5%.

targets.expr: (rate(ifHCInMulticastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInBroadcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval])) / (rate(ifHCInUcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInMulticastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval]) + rate(ifHCInBroadcastPkts{job="snmp-pfsense", ifAlias=~"$interface", ifAlias!=""}[$__rate_interval])) * 100

panels.title: Broadcast/multicast share of inbound packets (per VLAN)

panels.description: rx+tx drops and rx+tx errors on the physical NICs of the five node_exporter hosts (nas/ds-1/ds-2/dockermaster/pfsense) — complements the SNMP routing-plane discards with the host plane that is invisible today (pfSense ix0 has live tx drops).
panels.title: Host NIC drops & errors (node_exporter, all hosts)

panels.description: Carrier (link up/down) transitions per host NIC over the window — any non-zero increase is a flapping cable/port/NIC that SNMP ifOperStatus polling can miss between scrapes; currently 0 across all real devices.
panels.title: Link flaps — carrier changes (host NICs)

panels.description: Instant table of per-VLAN MTU (all currently 1500) so an accidental MTU change that silently causes fragmentation/blackholing — invisible on throughput graphs — is immediately obvious.
panels.title: Interface MTU (per VLAN) — config sanity
Pi-hole queries in window by instance

panels.description: True per-Pi-hole DNS throughput from the rolling 1-minute gauge, replacing the meaningless cumulative pihole_query_count line.
panels.title: Live DNS QPS per Pi-hole

panels.description: Live resolver-efficiency SLI; a falling cache-hit ratio signals cache flushing, TTL=0 abuse, or a unique-name flood.
panels.title: Cache-hit ratio per Pi-hole (live)

panels.description: Validates that each resolver returned a real parseable answer, catching answer-level failures the port-up probe_success timeline cannot detect.
panels.title: Internal DNS probe — answer succeeded (validity matrix)

panels.description: Per-instance resolution-failure ratio that surfaces HA divergence (pihole-2 ~36% vs pihole-1 ~19%) from a misbehaving upstream.
panels.title: Resolution failure share per Pi-hole (NXDOMAIN/SERVFAIL/REFUSED %)

panels.description: Where answers actually originate (cache ~671k vs blocklist ~9k vs forwarded 192.168.4.1 ~5.4k), revealing resolver dependency and upstream-lookup cost.
panels.title: Answer source split — cache vs blocklist vs forwarded upstream

panels.description: Record-type distribution; AAAA share reveals IPv6 adoption and HTTPS/SVCB share reveals DoH/ECH lookups, with always-zero types filtered out.
panels.title: Query type mix (A / AAAA / HTTPS / PTR …)

panels.description: Answers by outcome (IP/CNAME good vs NXDOMAIN/NODATA); a rising failure share is a direct resolution-quality degradation signal absent from the status donut.
panels.title: Reply outcome mix (resolution quality)

panels.description: Per-instance gravity size over time, surfacing failed gravity updates (drop to 0) and HA parity drift (pihole-2 carries 83068 vs 81382 elsewhere).
panels.title: Blocklist (gravity) size drift across the 3 Pi-holes

panels.description: Splits DNS lookup latency by phase so a slow request phase (upstream timeout) is distinguishable from a slow connect (reachability), turning one opaque number into a diagnosis.
panels.title: External DNS probe phase breakdown (connect / request / resolve)

panels.description: Per-VLAN firewall block rate (v4+v6, in+out) so you can see at a glance which VLAN is being dropped right now — impossible with the global pflog panels.
panels.title: Block rate by VLAN (pkts/s)

panels.description: Per-VLAN blocked share of total packets — volume-normalized anomaly signal so a quiet VLAN suddenly dropping a large fraction stands out even at low pps (clamp_min guards 0/0).
panels.title: Block-vs-pass ratio by VLAN (%)

panels.description: Single-glance verdict tile naming the worst-offending VLAN and its current block rate, mirroring the dashboard's verdict-strip pattern (set textMode=value_and_name).
panels.title: Top-blocked VLAN (now)

panels.description: Byte volume of blocked traffic per VLAN (v4+v6, in+out) — catches large-payload floods/exfil that packet rate alone would miss; pairs with the per-VLAN packet-rate panel.
panels.title: Blocked bytes by VLAN (bytes/s)

panels.description: Inbound-only block rate on igc0/igc1 (WAN1/WAN2) — the classic unsolicited-internet-traffic / scan / DDoS SLI that the global pflog panels fold away.
panels.title: WAN inbound blocks — internet attack surface (pkts/s)

panels.description: Protocol split of all blocked packets — a sudden IPv6 surge flags an ICMPv6/RA misconfig or v6-only scan that v4-centric monitoring misses (only the IoT VLAN carries IPv6 here).
panels.title: IPv4 vs IPv6 blocked traffic (pkts/s)

options.content: ℹ️ **Known false-positive:** `rustdesk.home` / `rustdesk-relay.home` report `probe_success=0` on plain-GET probes because they are **WebSocket-only** upstreams (they answer `101 Switching Protocols`). They are **excluded** from the *Probes Up %* tile and *Per-target availability*. See memory `blackbox-rproxy WebSocket false positive`.

panels.description: Per-service uptime over the selected window via avg_over_time(probe_success) — the core reachability SLI missing today (e.g. blackbox-dns 192.168.4.1:53 ~85%, rundeck/vault/keycloak ~93-94% intermittent drops, rustdesk WS excluded).
panels.title: Per-target availability % (window SLA)

panels.description: Dedicated liveness timeline for the 7 internal hosts/gateways in the blackbox-icmp job (192.168.4.1, .16.2, .4.236, .48.44-46, .7.11) — clean view that complements pfSense's WAN-only gateway RTT and is legible vs the buried all-jobs matrix.
panels.title: ICMP reachability — internal LAN & gateways (UP matrix)

panels.description: quantile_over_time on the probe_duration gauge (blackbox exposes no histogram buckets) surfaces tail latency that the existing raw 'Probe duration' panel hides — a chronically slow target reads differently from one slow scrape; rustdesk WS excluded.
panels.title: Probe latency percentiles (p50 / p95 / p99)

panels.description: Per-phase HTTP timing isolates WHICH stage is slow — e.g. calibre shows resolve~4000ms while tls/processing/transfer are sub-30ms, a DNS-resolution stall invisible in the aggregate probe_duration the dashboard graphs (5 phases × 15 HTTP targets).
panels.title: HTTP probe phase breakdown (resolve/connect/tls/processing/transfer)

panels.description: True internal-LAN/inter-VLAN latency (verified 192.168.4.1=0.91ms, .16.2=0.87ms, .4.236=0.65ms) that pfSense's WAN-only gateway RTT cannot show; spikes flag VLAN routing or NIC saturation.
panels.title: ICMP RTT to internal hosts & gateways

panels.description: Authoritative positive signal for the rustdesk WS false-positive: the blackbox-tcp job raw-connects all 5 hbbr/hbbs ports (21115-21119, all UP) — turns the static caveat note into a green proof while the WS-only HTTP probe stays excluded from the % tiles.
panels.title: RustDesk relay/signal TCP reachability

panels.description: Historical per-vhost status transitions (deduped with max by instance across the http/rproxy jobs) so a transient 5xx or a self-healing 0/probe-failure leaves a trace the existing instant status table loses; distinguishes a flapping backend from a steady outage.
panels.title: HTTP status code over time

panels.description: Per-service uptime for the ingress/DNS path (nginx-rproxy, cloudflared x3, pihole x3, vault x3); restart series fires on each process restart and is the SAME expr to wire into the dashboard-wide annotation (changes(process_start_time_seconds...)>0, iconColor orange, tag {{job}}).
panels.title: Ingress/DNS path uptime & restarts

panels.description: Count of ingress/DNS-path process restarts in the last 24h; non-zero correlates with blackbox-probe and throughput dips on this dashboard.
panels.title: Service restarts — last 24h

panels.description: Instant table of current uptime per ingress/DNS-path service (job/instance); low values flag a recent restart that explains a probe/throughput dip.
panels.title: Per-service uptime & last restart

title: 🌐 Internet & Network — Overview
